items.title: Maarten Paes en zijn American Dream: 'Ik word beter van deze stap'
items.summary: Jarenlang gold hij als één van de grootste keeperstalenten van Nederland. Maarten Paes (23) stond onder de lat bij Jong Oranje en werd gelinkt aan het Nederlands elftal. Maar sinds deze winter woont hij in Texas. Een gesprek over gesponsorde barbecues, keepen in The Oven en één van de meest opvallende transfers van dit seizoen. 'Je leert heel andere dingen dan in de Eredivisie.'

items.title: Spurs striker Kolo Muani 'fine' after car accident
items.summary: Randal Kolo Muani is "fine" after a "minor" car accident and should be available for Spurs' Champions League match at Eintracht Frankfurt, says Thomas Frank.

items.title: Frank’s injury woes mount as Tottenham prepare for vital Champions League tie
items.summary: Porro and Van de Ven join lengthy injury list Kolo Muani unharmed after M25 tyre blowout Thomas Frank’s preparations for the Champions League game at Eintracht Frankfurt on Wednesday night have been rocked by injuries to Pedro Porro and Micky van de Ven – and a car crash affecting Randal Kolo Muani and Wilson Odobert. The Tottenham manager reported that Kolo Muani and Odobert were OK after pictures emerged on social media of them standing by a black Ferrari with significant damage to its front end. Continue reading...

items.title: Mourinho goedgemutst richting weerzien met 'een van mijn favoriete spelers'
items.summary: Woensdagavond staat er een echte Europese klassieker op het programma in Lissabon: Benfica – Real Madrid. José Mourinho staat tegenover zijn oude club, en in de Madrileense dug-out zit met Álvaro Arbeloa iemand voor wie de Special One veel respect heeft.

items.title: Abraham joins Villa 1 day after Besiktas transfer
items.summary: Tammy Abraham has signed for Aston Villa from Besiktas just one day after he joined the Turkish club a permanent transfer from Roma.

items.title: 7 Greek fans die in crash en route to UEL game
items.summary: Seven fans of Greek soccer club PAOK Salonika died in a minibus crash in Romania while traveling to a Europa League game at Lyon, Greece's government confirmed Tuesday.

items.title: Rosenior squashes Palmer to Man United reports
items.summary: Chelsea head coach Liam Rosenior said there is no point in discussing "unrealistic" reports Cole Palmer wants to join Manchester United.

items.title: Spalletti levert bewijs aan na uithaal Conte: 'Dacht dat het AI was'
items.summary: Juventus-trainer Luciano Spalletti heeft gereageerd op de uithaal van Napoli-trainer Antonio Conte. Laatstgenoemde was boos, omdat Spalletti zijn ploeg de voormalig kampioen van Italië had genoemd in plaats van de huidige.

items.title: Spurs mist Van de Ven, duo wel van de partij na auto-ongeluk
items.summary: Micky van de Ven kan Tottenham Hotspur woensdagavond niet van dienst zijn in het Champions League-treffen op bezoek bij Eintracht Frankfurt. Dat heeft Tottenham-manager Thomas Frank dinsdagavond verteld. Positief nieuws: Van de Ven mankeert niks ernstigs.

items.title: Premier League has the power but still faces reckoning with European giants | Jonathan Wilson
items.summary: English clubs stroll through Champions League group phase but fatigue tends to take its toll by the spring Has there been a great game in the Champions League group stage this season? Probably not. Even if there had been, it almost certainly didn’t mean all that much. But that’s the way of the modern game: an extremely protracted clearing of the throat before the real business begins. Uefa will proudly tell the world that only six teams have nothing to play for in the final round of games on Wednesday, but whether it was worth 126 games to get to the mild peril of Napoli or Club Brugge possibly going out, or the questionable thrill of finding out whether Tottenham or Atalanta will have to endure the playoff round, is debatable. Continue reading...

items.title: Dit kan Go Ahead Eagles verwachten van Braga
items.summary: Go Ahead Eagles treft met Braga een tegenstander die de weg omhoog gevonden heeft. De Portugezen hebben na een lastige start een uitstekende positie in de Europa League en pakken steeds meer punten in de competitie. Analyse van een balbezitsploeg met Pep Guardiola-invloeden.

items.title: Conte reunion a sharp reminder of Chelsea's fall
items.summary: Chelsea have not won the Premier League since Antonio Conte led them to the title in 2017 - and now they face the Italian's Napoli side in the Champions League.

items.title: Villa sign striker Abraham for £18.25m
items.summary: England striker Tammy Abraham completes an £18.25m return to Aston Villa from Turkish side Besiktas.

items.title: Spurs forward Kolo Muani ‘fine’ after 'minor' car accident
items.summary: Tottenham forward Randal Kolo Muani was involved in a “minor” car accident on Tuesday, but is "fine", according to head coach Thomas Frank and is expected to feature in Wednesday's Champions League clash at Eintracht Frankfurt.

items.title: Arne Slot says PSG European exit helped Liverpool win Premier League
items.summary: Manager wants players ‘to improve in both boxes’ Liverpool without Konaté and Gomez against Qarabag Arne Slot has said an early Champions League exit may have contributed to Liverpool winning the Premier League title last year but said his team must improve in both boxes to secure silverware this season. Slot has faced renewed criticism after his side’s 13-game unbeaten run ended at Bournemouth on Saturday, leaving the reigning champions sixth in the Premier League. Continue reading...

items.title: Cardiff, Lincoln lead & Huddersfield score from 50 YARDS | EFL on Sky LIVE!
'The first person I called' - Koepka reveals Tiger role in PGA Tour return
items.summary: Brooks Koepka revealed the first person he called when he was released from his LIV contract was Tiger Woods, to discuss his return to the PGA Tour.

items.title: What happens next after latest link between heading and brain disease?
items.summary: BBC Sport explores the ramifications of the verdict that heading footballs "likely" contributed to Gordon McQueen's death.

items.title: 'Stone Cold' Brook smashes Sri Lanka - and imaginary beers - after torrid winter
items.summary: Harry Brook's winter has been dogged by unwanted headlines.

items.title: WSL2 minimum pay for under-23s less than national living wage for typical full-time job
items.summary: WSL says it is committed to increasing pay floors Clubs can be docked points for breaching salary cap Players aged under 23 in Women’s Super League 2 are not guaranteed to be paid the equivalent of the national living wage for a typical full-time worker annually, despite a large pay increase for the division’s lowest-paid players after the introduction of minimum salaries this season. WSL2 clubs must pay players aged 21 and 22 a minimum of £22,200 and those aged 18 to 20 at least £17,500. Regulations state they must receive a minimum “contact time” of 20 hours a week excluding matchdays and mealtimes. For players aged 23 and over, the minimum salary is £26,900. Continue reading...
